card_description: Mani (Aramaic: "Jewel/precious one") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the gnostic tradition, Mani is 3rd century prophet; founder of Manichaeism. PKD drew on Manichaean cosmic dualism of light versus darkness. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick proposes that the "psyche" of the Artifact (Form II) was slain by Christ during the First Advent (c."

linked_segments.summary: Dick proposes that the "psyche" of the Artifact (Form II) was slain by Christ during the First Advent (c. A.D. 70 at Masada/Temple Fall), and it has since been a "carcass" or "sarx" projecting a froze
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_132.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick refines his "2-Source" theory, identifying our world as a "projected mixture" or hologram of two hyper-realities: Form I (Palm Tree Garden/Heaven/Light) and Form II (Black Iron Prison/Hell/Night)
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_131.txt

reference.significance: PKD drew on Manichaean cosmic dualism of light versus darkness
